title: Executive Order 12217—Federal Compliance With Fuel Use Prohibitions

flags.justification: Distributed responsibility across agency heads, with the Secretary of Energy providing guidance and assistance.
A detailed annual planning and reporting requirement, closely modeled on EO 12088.
Multiple related requirements (survey, planning, funding) all coherently serve one purpose.
References specific statutory titles (Titles II and III of the Fuel Use Act), well-anchored to external legal standards.
No incentive structure rewarding behavior contrary to the order's purpose is identifiable.
A standing compliance framework, evergreen by nature.
No existing oversight mechanism is removed.
Sec. 1-102(b) explicitly incorporates EO 12088's pollution-control compliance structure, inheriting that order's real external state/local approval mechanisms by reference.
Extends the already-established EO 12088 compliance template to a new domain (fuel use); not a novel authority claim.
No second agency's competing mandate is implicated.
Sec. 1-501 defers all exemption authority entirely to the underlying statute's own provisions, creating no separate order-level exemption path.

notes: Validation batch (positive). Structural weight: 0/18 = 0.0%. Extends the well-designed EO 12088 pollution-compliance model to fuel-use requirements.
